assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes notification types including 'Upgrade available event', 'Scheduled upgrade notifications', 'Minor version at/near EoS', and 'New patch with COS milestone' covering all required categories

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response assigns urgency levels using color coding and symbols: security patches get 🟡 Evaluate (higher urgency), scheduled upgrades get 🟠 Prepare, while upgrade available gets 📘 Info (lowest urgency)

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states EoS warnings require planning upgrades with specific timelines: 30+ days = plan upgrade, <30 days = urgent schedule upgrade, and provides commands for handling EoS situations

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides specific pre-flight checklist commands for scheduled upgrades including checking pod status, PDBs, deprecated APIs, and maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response mentions security patches marked 'critical' or 'high' require immediate action and includes them in the urgent response category requiring <2 hour response time

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Upgrade Available' notifications require 'None (unless manual upgrade desired)' action and are marked as 📘 Info with 'No deadline'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response provides Cloud Monitoring alert policy examples and mentions setting up Pub/Sub subscription for programmatic handling of cluster events

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release notes as a source for understanding version changes, focusing instead on notification handling and triage processes
